description:
**Milestone:** Trajectory Store & Learning Flywheel

Populate the existing ctx.siblingReflections placeholder (StateContext line 124) by calling trajectoryStore.loadSiblings() during StateContext hydration. Then inject the loaded trajectories into the agent system prompt via buildSystemPrompt() in dynamic-agent-executor.ts.

The sibling context should be formatted concisely: for each trajectory, show domain, complexity, what was attempted, what succeeded/failed, and any key learnings. Cap at 3 siblings and ~500 tokens total to avoid bloating prompts. Only inject trajectories where verified=true (successfully merged) to prevent propagating bad patterns.

**Files to Modify:**
- apps/server/src/services/lead-engineer-service.ts
- apps/server/src/services/dynamic-agent-executor.ts

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] StateContext.siblingReflections populated from trajectory store during ExecuteProcessor context hydration
- [ ] buildSystemPrompt() renders sibling trajectories under a 'Lessons from Similar Features' section when present
- [ ] Only verified=true trajectories are injected
- [ ] Maximum 3 siblings, ~500 token cap enforced
- [ ] npm run build:server passes
- [ ] npm run test:server passes

**Complexity:** small
